title: Introducing OpenAI Presence
author: OpenAI
why_it_matters: OpenAI frames production agents as job-scoped systems with policies, guardrails, approved actions, simulations, evaluations, escalations and a Codex-powered improvement loop.
actionable_takeaway: Write a job card for each automation before expanding permissions: goal, knowledge, system access, approved actions, approval points, escalation conditions and validation.

title: How news organizations are using AI to advance their vital missions
author: OpenAI
why_it_matters: The newsroom examples show practical AI patterns: public-record search, transcript scoring, style checking, knowledge agents, archive retrieval and Slack-based decision support.
actionable_takeaway: Evolve AI Radar from a summary into a lead-scoring loop: scan public sources, structure evidence, score signals, apply human judgment and save reusable cards.

title: GPT-5.6: Frontier intelligence that scales with your ambition
author: OpenAI
why_it_matters: GPT-5.6 emphasizes coding-agent efficiency, Programmatic Tool Calling and multi-agent ultra-style parallelism, shifting attention from single answers to orchestrated work.
actionable_takeaway: For repeated tool-heavy workflows, benchmark model choice plus orchestration by success quality, token use, latency, cost and evidence completeness.

title: Model guidance for GPT-5.6
author: OpenAI Developers
why_it_matters: The guide gives migration guidance for Sol, Terra and Luna, reasoning effort, cache cost and Programmatic Tool Calling with allowed callers and program outputs.
actionable_takeaway: Use Sol for frontier work, Terra for balanced tasks, Luna for high-volume routines, and only adopt PTC after measuring final answer quality against current flow.

title: ChatGPT desktop app experience updates
author: OpenAI Help Center
why_it_matters: The July 16 release brings clearer Chat / Work / Codex desktop layout, unified recents, Projects in desktop and cross-device Work continuation while keeping Codex separate.
actionable_takeaway: Route tasks deliberately: Chat for questions, Work for finished deliverables, Codex for file changes and reviewable diffs, Sites for public artifacts.

title: Work with Codex from anywhere
author: OpenAI
why_it_matters: Codex mobile remote control formalizes iPhone-to-host supervision with approvals, screenshots, terminal output, diffs, test results and relay-based connectivity.
actionable_takeaway: Keep iPhone as capture and steering surface; reserve final diff review, tests, commit, push and deployment for Mac.

title: New Copilot usage metrics impact dashboard
author: GitHub Changelog
why_it_matters: GitHub groups Copilot adoption by Code-first, Agent-first, Multi-agent/Copilot app and Passive cohorts, making AI maturity observable beyond active user counts.
actionable_takeaway: Add maturity metrics to AI Radar monthly review: entry, execution, validation, reuse, failed runs and published Ready status.

title: Gemini 3.6 Flash is now available in GitHub Copilot
author: GitHub Changelog
why_it_matters: Copilot keeps adding task-oriented models across IDEs, CLI, cloud agent and app, reinforcing model choice as an operational routing layer.
actionable_takeaway: Define default routing by task: cheap model for extraction, stronger model for synthesis, highest reasoning only for high-risk or hard-to-validate work.

title: Copilot users can now see AI credits used per billing cycle
author: GitHub Changelog
why_it_matters: Users can see actual AI credit consumption even without an individual budget, making cost visible to the person doing the work.
actionable_takeaway: Track source card count, failed retries, deployment failures and knowledge reuse so personal AI cost can be evaluated against output value.

title: GitHub Copilot app now available in the usage metrics API
author: GitHub Changelog
why_it_matters: The Copilot app now has distinct active user, session, request, prompt and token metrics in usage reports instead of being hidden inside generic totals.
actionable_takeaway: Separate Codex app, mobile, browser, CLI and automation metrics instead of flattening all AI work into one count.

title: Copilot code review customization and configurability improvements
author: GitHub Changelog
why_it_matters: Configurable review behavior points toward repo-specific review policy rather than generic agent feedback.
actionable_takeaway: Keep review instructions close to the repo and let automation check project-specific rules before public changes.

title: How agents are transforming work
author: OpenAI
why_it_matters: OpenAI describes work shifting from short interactions to delegated long-horizon tasks and parallel agent orchestration across technical and non-technical teams.
actionable_takeaway: For long work, split into independent tracks only when each track has its own evidence and merge point; avoid one oversized long session.

title: ChatGPT Work and Plugin Directory release notes
author: OpenAI Help Center
why_it_matters: ChatGPT Work and the Plugin Directory put apps, skills, templates, Sites and Codex in the same workbench, raising permission and writeback governance needs.
actionable_takeaway: Keep external connectors opt-in: read first, write only with current-turn authorization and explicit destination.

title: Shortcuts User Guide for iPhone and iPad
author: Apple Support
why_it_matters: Shortcuts remains the stable iPhone automation surface for capture, reminders, transformations and low-risk dispatch around AI workflows.
actionable_takeaway: Use Shortcuts for intake and structured handoff, not for irreversible public publishing or private-data leakage.
